gomodel:run-figure2-004/fshr1/molecular_.snippet: fshr-1 annotated to G protein-coupled receptor activity; signaling-receptor MF consistent with apical receptor glyph in Lumen.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/fshr1/part_of.snippet: fshr-1 required for defense against Gram-negative bacteria (P. aeruginosa).

gomodel:run-figure2-004/fshr1/occurs_in.snippet: fshr-1 localizes to plasma membrane; figure shows it as a transmembrane receptor at the intestinal apical/luminal surface.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/tir1/molecular_f.snippet: TIR-1/SARM1 acts as a signaling adaptor upstream of the NSY-1 p38 MAPK module.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/tir1/part_of.snippet: tir-1 required for defense response to bacterium in the intestine.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/nsy1/molecular_f.snippet: NSY-1 is the MAPKKK of the C. elegans p38 module.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/nsy1/part_of.snippet: NSY-1 functions in the p38 MAPK cascade for innate immunity.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/sek1/molecular_f.snippet: SEK-1 is the MAPKK of the C. elegans p38 module.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/sek1/part_of.snippet: SEK-1 functions in the p38 MAPK cascade for innate immunity.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/pmk1/molecular_f.snippet: PMK-1 is the p38 MAP kinase of the intestinal immune module.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/pmk1/part_of.snippet: PMK-1 functions in the p38 MAPK cascade for innate immunity.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/hsp60/molecular_.snippet: HSP-60 is a mitochondrial chaperonin; its induction is the readout of UPRmt.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/hsp60/part_of.snippet: hsp-60 induction is a hallmark of the mitochondrial unfolded protein response (UPRmt).

gomodel:run-figure2-004/hsp60/occurs_in.snippet: HSP-60 localizes to mitochondria; figure places it inside the Mitochondria organelle.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/atfs1/molecular_.snippet: ATFS-1 is the master bZIP transcription factor of the UPRmt.
gomodel:run-figure2-004/atfs1/molecular_.term_label: DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-004/atfs1/part_of.snippet: ATFS-1 drives the transcriptional UPRmt program (incl. ESRE/stress genes shown into the Nucleus).

gomodel:run-figure2-004/atfs1/occurs_in.snippet: ATFS-1 accumulates in the nucleus to activate target genes.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/hif1/molecular_f.snippet: HIF-1 is the hypoxia-inducible bHLH-PAS transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/hif1/part_of.snippet: HIF-1 mediates the response to hypoxia; activated by mtROS in this figure.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/aak2/molecular_f.snippet: aak-2 is the catalytic alpha subunit of AMPK (figure label 'AMPK').

gomodel:run-figure2-004/aak2/part_of.snippet: mtROS --> AMPK (aak-2), placing AAK-2 in the oxidative stress response arm.
gomodel:run-figure2-004/aak2/part_of.justification: Figure shows mitochondrial ROS (mtROS) activating AMPK/aak-2 as part of the cellular oxidative-stress response feeding nuclear stress genes. aak-2 had no oxidative-stress BP in its retrieved annotation set, so the process is inferred from the figure context; curator to confirm exact GO BP.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/daf16/molecular_.term_label: DNA-binding transcription activator activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-004/daf16/part_of.snippet: DAF-16 --> cellular stress response genes (regulation of transcription).
gomodel:run-figure2-004/daf16/part_of.justification: DAF-16/FOXO is a sequence-specific RNA Pol II transcription factor; the figure shows it driving 'cellular stress response genes' in the nucleus. A positive regulation-of-transcription BP was not in the retrieved annotation subset, so the transcriptional role is asserted by inference for the TF-endpoint pattern.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/daf16/occurs_in.snippet: DAF-16 box with arrow into the Nucleus compartment.
gomodel:run-figure2-004/daf16/occurs_in.justification: DAF-16/FOXO is a canonical nuclear transcription factor (nucleus entailed by its DNA-binding TF activity); figure shows it acting on nuclear gene sets. No nucleus CC was present in the retrieved annotation subset.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/skn1/molecular_f.snippet: SKN-1/Nrf2 acts as an RNA Pol II transcriptional activator.
gomodel:run-figure2-004/skn1/molecular_f.term_label: DNA-binding transcription activator activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-004/skn1/part_of.snippet: SKN-1/Nrf2 drives the oxidative/cellular stress response (figure: SKN-1 --> cellular stress response genes).

gomodel:run-figure2-004/skn1/occurs_in.snippet: SKN-1 box with arrow into the Nucleus compartment.
gomodel:run-figure2-004/skn1/occurs_in.justification: SKN-1/Nrf2 is a canonical nuclear transcription factor (nucleus entailed by its DNA-binding TF activity); figure shows it acting on nuclear gene sets. No nucleus CC was present in the retrieved annotation subset.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/hsf1/molecular_f.snippet: HSF-1 is the heat-shock transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/hsf1/part_of.snippet: HSF-1 drives the cytoprotective heat-shock/cellular stress response (figure: HSF-1 --> cellular stress response genes).

gomodel:run-figure2-004/hsf1/occurs_in.snippet: HSF-1 box with arrow into the Nucleus compartment.
gomodel:run-figure2-004/hsf1/occurs_in.justification: HSF-1 is a canonical nuclear transcription factor (nucleus entailed by its DNA-binding TF activity); figure shows it acting on nuclear gene sets. No nucleus CC was present in the retrieved annotation subset.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/atf7/molecular_f.snippet: ATF-7 is a bZIP transcription factor downstream of PMK-1.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/atf7/part_of.snippet: PMK-1-phosphorylated ATF-7 activates antimicrobial defence genes (figure: ATF-7 --> antimicrobial defence genes).

gomodel:run-figure2-004/zip4/molecular_f.snippet: ZIP-4 is a bZIP transcription factor.
gomodel:run-figure2-004/zip4/molecular_f.term_label: DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-004/zip4/part_of.snippet: zip-4 functions in defense against Gram-negative bacteria.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/zip4/occurs_in.snippet: ZIP-4 transcription factor acts in the nucleus.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/cebp1/molecular_.snippet: CEBP-1 is a bZIP transcription factor.
gomodel:run-figure2-004/cebp1/molecular_.term_label: DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-004/cebp1/part_of.snippet: cebp-1 functions in defense against Gram-negative bacteria.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/cebp1/occurs_in.snippet: CEBP-1/2 box in the intestinal-cell nuclear regulator group.
gomodel:run-figure2-004/cebp1/occurs_in.justification: CEBP-1 is a bZIP transcription factor (nucleus entailed by its DNA-binding TF activity); figure places it among nuclear regulators. No nucleus CC was present in the retrieved annotation subset.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/cebp2/molecular_.snippet: CEBP-2 is a bZIP transcription factor.
gomodel:run-figure2-004/cebp2/molecular_.term_label: DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-004/cebp2/part_of.snippet: cebp-2 functions with zip-2 in antibacterial defence (figure: CEBP-1/2 --> ZIP-2).

gomodel:run-figure2-004/cebp2/occurs_in.snippet: CEBP-2 transcription factor acts in the nucleus.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/zip2/molecular_f.snippet: ZIP-2 is the bZIP transcription factor that activates antimicrobial genes upon P. aeruginosa exposure.
gomodel:run-figure2-004/zip2/molecular_f.term_label: DNA-binding transcription factor activity, RNA polymerase II-specific

gomodel:run-figure2-004/zip2/part_of.snippet: ZIP-2 drives antimicrobial defence gene expression (figure: ZIP-2 --> antimicrobial defence genes in Nucleus).

gomodel:run-figure2-004/nhr86/molecular_.snippet: NHR-86/HNF-4 is a nuclear-receptor transcription factor.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/nhr86/part_of.snippet: NHR-86/HNF-4 activates antibacterial defence genes (figure: NHR-86 --> antimicrobial defence genes).

gomodel:run-figure2-004/tir1/has_input/C.snippet: Pyocyanin (green hexagon, Lumen) --> TIR-1/SARM1 pointed arrow crossing the membrane. Pyocyanin is the P. aeruginosa phenazine stimulus that triggers TIR-1-dependent p38 immunity. ChEBI:27974 (pyocyanin) identification to be verified by curator.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/nhr86/has_input/.snippet: PCN (pyocyanin, green hexagon, Lumen) --> NHR-86/HNF-4 pointed arrow. NHR-86 senses the bacterial phenazine to activate antibacterial genes. ChEBI:27974 (pyocyanin) identification to be verified by curator; edge flagged uncertain in transcription (conf 0.5).

gomodel:run-figure2-004/hif1/has_input/C.snippet: mtROS (label inside Mitochondria) --> HIF-1 pointed arrow. Mitochondrial reactive oxygen species activate HIF-1.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/aak2/has_input/C.snippet: mtROS (label inside Mitochondria) --> AMPK (aak-2) pointed arrow. Mitochondrial reactive oxygen species activate AMPK.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/hsf1/has_input/C.snippet: HSF-1 -- ROS box connector (direction/relation ambiguous, conf 0.35). Modeled as ROS being sensed by the HSF-1 stress-response arm; curator to verify direction.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/hsp60/has_input/.snippet: Pyoverdine (Lumen) sequesters iron; +Fe3+ accumulates in the Mitochondria organelle, driving mitochondrial stress and the UPRmt (hsp-60 induction). Iron(3+) routed as the mitochondrial iron-stress stimulus on the UPRmt responder HSP-60; curator to re-home if a dedicated mito-stress activity is preferred.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/tir1/causal/gomo.snippet: Figure collapses TIR-1 --> LRO stress (pH increase) --> NSY-1 into one edge. Pathogen effectors trigger TIR-1/SARM1 aggregation on lysosome-related organelles (LRO stress), which activates the MAPKKK NSY-1 of the p38 module. tir-1 indirectly positively regulates nsy-1 (via the LRO-stress intermediate). Predicate: indirectly positively regulates.

Lysosome-related organelle integrity suppresses TIR-1 aggregation to restrain toxic propagation of p38 innate immunity — corroborates that LRO stress controls TIR-1-dependent activation of the NSY-1/SEK-1/PMK-1 p38 cascade. Predicate: indirectly positively regulates.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/nsy1/causal/gomo.snippet: NSY-1 (MAPKKK) directly phosphorylates and activates SEK-1 (MAPKK); the two physically interact in the C. elegans p38 module. Predicate: directly positively regulates.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/sek1/causal/gomo.snippet: SEK-1 (MAPKK) directly phosphorylates and activates PMK-1 (p38 MAPK) in the intestinal innate-immunity cascade. Predicate: directly positively regulates.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/pmk1/causal/gomo.snippet: PMK-1 (p38) physically binds and directly phosphorylates the transcription factor ATF-7, converting it from a repressor to an activator of PMK-1-dependent antimicrobial genes. Predicate: directly positively regulates.
PMK-1 causally upstream, positive effect, of DAF-16 (grouped connector into the DAF-16/SKN-1/HSF-1 cluster).
gomodel:run-figure2-004/pmk1/causal/gomo.justification: Figure draws a grouped connector PMK-1 --> DAF-16/SKN-1/HSF-1 cluster (conf 0.65; exact targets/directness uncertain). PMK-1 p38 signaling promotes DAF-16/FOXO-dependent stress gene expression, but the figure does not resolve mechanism/directness and no specific PMID for a direct PMK-1->DAF-16 step was retrieved, so modeled as causally upstream, positive effect.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/pmk1/causal/gomo.snippet: PMK-1 causally upstream, positive effect, of SKN-1 (oxidative stress response arm).
gomodel:run-figure2-004/pmk1/causal/gomo.justification: Part of the grouped PMK-1 --> DAF-16/SKN-1/HSF-1 connector (conf 0.65). PMK-1 p38 signaling promotes SKN-1/Nrf2 oxidative-stress responses; the figure does not resolve directness and no specific PMID was retrieved here, so modeled as causally upstream, positive effect.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/pmk1/causal/gomo.snippet: PMK-1 causally upstream, positive effect, of HSF-1 (cellular stress response arm).
gomodel:run-figure2-004/pmk1/causal/gomo.justification: Part of the grouped PMK-1 --> DAF-16/SKN-1/HSF-1 connector (conf 0.65). PMK-1 p38 signaling is shown promoting the HSF-1 cytoprotective/stress program; directness not resolved by the figure and no specific PMID retrieved, so modeled as causally upstream, positive effect.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/hsp60/causal/gom.snippet: HSP-60 --> ATFS-1 pointed arrow (conf 0.65, flagged uncertain in transcription). Figure draws the mitochondrial UPRmt (marked by HSP-60) as upstream of ATFS-1. NOTE: canonically ATFS-1 is the master UPRmt TF that drives hsp-60 induction (i.e. ATFS-1 is usually upstream of HSP-60), so the drawn direction is flagged for curator verification. Predicate: causally upstream of, positive effect.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/zip4/causal/gomo.snippet: ZIP-4 --> ZIP-2 pointed arrow (conf 0.55, flagged uncertain). bZIP TF ZIP-4 promotes the ZIP-2-driven antimicrobial response; TF-to-TF regulation acts via transcription (intervening module), so modeled as indirectly positively regulates. Predicate: indirectly positively regulates.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/cebp1/causal/gom.snippet: CEBP-1/2 --> ZIP-2 pointed arrow (conf 0.55, flagged uncertain). bZIP TF CEBP-1 promotes the ZIP-2 antimicrobial program; TF-to-TF regulation via transcription, so modeled as indirectly positively regulates. Predicate: indirectly positively regulates.

gomodel:run-figure2-004/cebp2/causal/gom.snippet: CEBP-1/2 --> ZIP-2 pointed arrow (CEBP-2 secondary label, conf 0.5, flagged uncertain). CEBP-2 partners in the ZIP-2 antimicrobial response; TF-to-TF regulation via transcription, so modeled as indirectly positively regulates. Predicate: indirectly positively regulates.
